response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web-oriented engineer with 15+ years of total experience, but the role demands a senior C++ developer with industrial automation and embedded/IoT expertise. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; C++ does not even appear in his skills list. There is no evidence of multi-threaded C++ systems, MQTT, IEC 62443, message-broker-based industrial applications, or any industrial-automation domain knowledge — all of which are core to this position. His strengths lie in TypeScript/Node.js web platforms and event-driven web architectures, which are only listed as advantageous, not defining, skills for this role.
strengths: Broad polyglot background with demonstrated event-driven and distributed-systems thinking (Sociomantic high-throughput micro-services, GISCollective platform)
Node.js and React experience aligns with the listed advantageous skills and could support peripheral tooling work
Active mentoring and knowledge-sharing history (workshops, Refactoring Dojos, public talks) fits the team-mentorship expectation
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — roughly 6 months in 2014–2015 and one thesis project; the role requires 8–12 years of hands-on C++ with STL and concurrent programming
Zero exposure to industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850) or IEC 62443 secure-development lifecycle standards
No background in industrial automation, IoT embedded systems, or safety-critical/on-premises platform engineering, which are the core domain of the role
